summary.intent: Address two rounds of Codex AI code review feedback on PR #694 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing state synchronization issues in RatingGroup and TagGroup components plus a no-std compile issue in ars-collections.
summary.outcome: Both review rounds were fully addressed: all 6 first-pass threads and all 8 second-pass threads were fixed, replied to, and resolved. A second @codex review was triggered and completed. The fixes were committed (778533b3 for round 1, a second commit for round 2), force-pushed, and all local CI gates (xci-fast) passed green.

repo: The repo uses a `cargo xci-fast` command as a multi-step local CI gate covering fmt, check, clippy, unit tests, spec conformance, proptest state machines, error-variant checks, mutual-exclusion guards, and snapshot counts.
Commit messages require a `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer for Codex-reviewed changes.
The workflow uses `@codex review` comment triggers on PRs; Codex signals active review with an 'eyes' reaction and completion with a 'thumbs-up' reaction (or absence of eyes).
Mutation testing exclusions live in `.cargo/mutants.toml`; broad transition mutant exclusions should be replaced with direct regression tests rather than blanket suppression.
The repo uses `ars_i18n` plural formatting with one/other forms for localized labels.
Component prop-only attribute changes should emit `SyncProps` to propagate derived attribute updates.
No-std compliance is required for `ars-collections`; the broader `ars-components --no-default-features` path has pre-existing `alloc` import gaps in unrelated modules.

code.finding: Missing no-std-compatible import for key conversion; caused compile failure under --no-default-features.

RatingGroup needed to emit SyncProps on controlled/runtime prop changes, sync controlled/uncontrolled value through effective step and count clamp, clear hover state, and reconcile focus when count or disabled changes. Also needed typed value-change effect and latest requested value field instead of adapter callbacks on Props.

TagGroup needed SyncProps emission for item/selection/mode/disabled changes, mirroring items and mode into context, syncing controlled/uncontrolled selected keys, dropping stale or disabled focus, normalizing tabindex, and fixing selection normalization through a single helper. DeselectTag needed to guard against mutating selection for disabled tags.

workflow: When rebasing after a review cycle, the next push must use --force-with-lease since the branch tip diverges from the remote.
GitHub GraphQL API (`gh api graphql`) with `resolveReviewThread` mutation is used to programmatically resolve PR review threads.
Codex review polling strategy: watch the reaction on the trigger comment (eyes = active, thumbs-up or absence of eyes = done), then fetch all review threads via GraphQL to get new unresolved items.
Running focused test slices (`cargo test -p ars-components --lib data_display::rating_group::tests`) before the full xci-fast gate saves time during iterative fix cycles.
Filtering `cargo check` error output with `rg` is useful for distinguishing pre-existing failures from newly introduced ones in large workspaces.

friction: Codex review polling required repeated sleep+query cycles over several minutes with no push-based notification, requiring manual interval tuning.

The `ars-components --no-default-features` path has pre-existing `alloc` import failures in unrelated modules, making it hard to isolate whether the targeted fixes were clean without output filtering.
Force-push was required after rebasing the review response commit, adding friction to the push step.
Commit hooks ran on each commit and required waiting for completion before proceeding.
open_items: Pre-existing `alloc` import gaps in `ars-components` under `--no-default-features` were not fixed — only the RatingGroup/TagGroup/Key-specific errors were resolved.
The second @codex review pass was triggered and completed, but the transcript ends before confirming whether Codex gave final approval (thumbs-up) or whether any third-round issues emerged.
GitHub CI unit tests were still running at the end of the first review cycle — final CI green status on the remote was not confirmed in the transcript.
